name: Bioswale
maintenanceNeeds: Remove sediment and trash, maintain vegetation, inspect inlets after storms.
sourceNote: Source-backed planning range from EPA green infrastructure guidance and stormwater practice design references; calibrate locally before engineering use.

name: Rain Garden
maintenanceNeeds: Weed, mulch, replace plants, keep inflow clear, inspect after heavy storms.
sourceNote: Source-backed planning range from EPA and extension rain garden guidance; use soil tests for final design.

name: Permeable Pavement
notGoodFor: heavy truck routes without structural design
maintenanceNeeds: Vacuum sweep, prevent sediment clogging, inspect subbase drainage.
sourceNote: Source-backed planning range from EPA green infrastructure and permeable pavement practice references.

name: Tree Trench
maintenanceNeeds: Tree care, inlet cleaning, structural soil inspection, replace dead trees.
sourceNote: Source-backed planning range adapted from urban tree and bioretention stormwater references.

name: Retention Basin
maintenanceNeeds: Sediment removal, outlet inspection, vegetation management, embankment inspection.
sourceNote: Source-backed planning range from detention/retention basin design references and EPA stormwater practice guidance.

name: Wetland
notGoodFor: sites without baseflow or planting area
maintenanceNeeds: Manage invasive species, sediment, trash, outlet controls, and vegetation health.
sourceNote: Source-backed planning range from constructed wetland and EPA green infrastructure guidance.

name: Smart Storage Network
bestFor: places with limited surface space for large green infrastructure
areas where captured water could support drought reuse or irrigation
notGoodFor: places with no feasible underground storage space
areas with poor power or backup reliability
maintenanceNeeds: Inspect sensors, power backup, telemetry, smart valves, tank access, sediment, pumps, emergency bypasses, and slow-release settings.
sourceNote: Planning range combines underground detention, smart outlet control, and slow-release assumptions; verify with SWMM, local hydraulic models, and utility conflict review.
